research: RaBitQ (Gao & Long, SIGMOD 2024, arXiv:2405.12497) + ITQ (Gong & Lazebnik 2011). Core idea tested: a random orthogonal rotation before sign-binarization spreads each vector's info evenly across dims so every bit carries independent signal -> better binary codes. Implemented as a fast FWHT-based structured rotation (random sign-flip + Walsh-Hadamard, deterministic seed, same for base+query).
note: Rotation improves binary recall at every C with ZERO QPS cost (rotation is prep-time + a tiny per-query FWHT; scan is identical Hamming). Win is biggest at low C (stage-1 +2.4pts) -> reach target recall at smaller C -> faster funnel. 2 rounds suffice (rot x3 ~= x2). Modest on Cohere (already well-conditioned/compression-aware); larger on raw embeddings.
